properties: × Elyhordeum langei is not a widely documented or known species. However, its parent species, Hordeum (barley), has been studied for its therapeutic properties. Hordeum contains compounds such as selenium, which has antioxidant properties, and beta-glucans, which have been shown to stimulate the immune system. The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of Hordeum may contribute to its potential therapeutic benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic properties of × Elyhordeum langei, if it is indeed a valid species.
